surfaces.label: Adaptive asset planning
surfaces.summary: Build-time media, font, video, and 3D plans with explicit device budgets and fallbacks.
limitations: Runtime image optimization and dynamic Open Graph rendering are not released.

surfaces.label: Browser adapter lifecycle
surfaces.summary: Explicit mount, update, unmount, abort, lazy admission, reduced-motion, and cleanup for JavaScript libraries.
limitations: Adapters execute with page privileges and are a lifecycle contract, not a JavaScript sandbox.

surfaces.label: Cloudflare application runtime
surfaces.summary: A conformant Cloudflare Workers runtime for portable PliegoRS server applications.
limitations: The current Cloudflare package serves the static documentation site; it is not the PliegoRS application runtime.

surfaces.label: Loaders, actions, sessions, and cache
surfaces.summary: Request-scoped data loading, progressive mutations, session boundaries, and explicit distributed cache policies.
limitations: PliegoRS currently provides no production server actions, sessions, ISR, or multi-instance invalidation contract.

surfaces.label: Deterministic static generation
surfaces.summary: Typed documents, routes, heads, assets, staged publication, ownership ledgers, and exact-set verification.
limitations: The released server serves static output only and does not provide request-time rendering.

surfaces.label: Dynamic and streamed SSR
surfaces.summary: Request-time synchronous, asynchronous, and streamed server rendering with explicit boundaries.
limitations: No request-time SSR or streaming API is part of the current public release.

surfaces.label: Full-stack route graph
surfaces.summary: Nested layouts, typed dynamic parameters, route handlers, middleware, and authored error boundaries.
limitations: Unreleased source implements the bounded core grammar, collision admission, deterministic digest, parameter matching, and HTTP method dispatch.
Layouts, middleware, error boundaries, generated parameter types, fuzzing, and host conformance remain incomplete.

surfaces.label: Hyphae verified protocol boundary
surfaces.summary: Optional protocol v2 attestations, authority policy, and type-gated verified replay for durable applications.
limitations: No production Hyphae gateway or durability service is claimed by this repository.

surfaces.label: Native HTTP runtime
surfaces.summary: A production Axum, Hyper, Tower, and Tokio host with bounded requests, cancellation, streaming, and graceful shutdown.
limitations: The unreleased source is a lifecycle foundation, not a production-conformant server release.
Rendering, middleware, OpenTelemetry, load evidence, and the threat-model corpus remain incomplete; tiny_http remains limited to development and static preview commands.

surfaces.label: OpenSDK browser and tooling planes
surfaces.summary: Browser framework and JSON-RPC/MCP conformance surfaces implemented on main for evaluation.
limitations: The preview is not published on crates.io and its RFC and ADR decisions remain pending.

surfaces.label: OpenSDK build plane
surfaces.summary: Typed WebAssembly Component admission, resource budgets, capabilities, effect receipts, and conformance fixtures.
limitations: pliego-sdk 0.1.0-preview.1 exists only on main and is not part of the released 0.0.2 package set.

surfaces.label: OpenSDK server plane
surfaces.summary: Language-neutral server extensions aligned with standard WASI HTTP semantics and explicit PliegoRS capabilities.
limitations: The current pliego:http preview buffers complete request and response bodies and must not be stabilized.

surfaces.label: Pliego Build Output Contract
surfaces.summary: A provider-neutral, digest-bound, executable output contract shared by independent deployment hosts.
limitations: RFC-007 is Draft and no native plus Cloudflare conformance corpus has closed the stability gate.

surfaces.label: Production server observability
surfaces.summary: OpenTelemetry HTTP spans, metrics, structured logs, runtime diagnostics, and bounded support evidence.
limitations: Build and browser evidence do not constitute request-time server observability.

surfaces.label: Rust and WebAssembly UI runtime
surfaces.summary: Escaped views, typed components, reactive ownership, event folds, resume actions, and verified DOM cleanup.
limitations: Current release-blocking browser lifecycle evidence is Chromium; Firefox and Safari remain candidates.

surfaces.label: Native and OCI application deployment
surfaces.summary: A portable Linux and OCI server artifact with runtime capabilities, rolling deployment, and rollback evidence.
limitations: Released CLI platform archives are development and build tools, not full-stack application server artifacts.

surfaces.label: Typed content collections
surfaces.summary: Bounded Markdown, JSON, and TOML discovery with typed schemas, safe paths, and deterministic diagnostics.
limitations: Content loading is build-time and is not a request-scoped server data loader.
